697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bc6.leadin: This poem ends with the speaker declaring: “Like men we’ll face the murderous, cowardly pack, / Pressed to the wall, dying, but fighting back!” For 10 points each:
697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bc6.leadin_sanitized: This poem ends with the speaker declaring: "Like men we'll face the murderous, cowardly pack, / Pressed to the wall, dying, but fighting back!" For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this sonnet by Claude McKay which says of the title action “let it not be like hogs / Hunted and penned in an inglorious spot.”

McKay’s “If We Must Die” was cited as “one of the most famous poems ever written” by this poet, who wrote a poem describing pool players at the Golden Shovel who “lurk late,” “strike straight,” “jazz June,” and “die soon.”
Along with Countee Cullen and Langston Hughes, McKay was a leading poet of this African-American cultural golden age named for a New York City neighborhood.
parts_sanitized: Identify this sonnet by Claude McKay which says of the title action "let it not be like hogs / Hunted and penned in an inglorious spot."

McKay's "If We Must Die" was cited as "one of the most famous poems ever written" by this poet, who wrote a poem describing pool players at the Golden Shovel who "lurk late," "strike straight," "jazz June," and "die soon."
Along with Countee Cullen and Langston Hughes, McKay was a leading poet of this African-American cultural golden age named for a New York City neighborhood.
answers_sanitized: Gwendolyn Brooks (That poem is "We Real Cool.")

697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bd7.leadin: These laws were intended to crack down on a German nationalist movement of “student festivals.” For 10 points each:
697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bd7.leadin_sanitized: These laws were intended to crack down on a German nationalist movement of "student festivals." For 10 points each:
parts: Name these repressive laws issued by Klemens von Metternich after the assassination of conservative playwright August von Kotzebue by a nationalist student.
The Carlsbad Decrees were repealed after a revolution in Germany in this year that also saw the overthrow of several monarchies in France and Central Europe.

parts_sanitized: Name these repressive laws issued by Klemens von Metternich after the assassination of conservative playwright August von Kotzebue by a nationalist student.
The Carlsbad Decrees were repealed after a revolution in Germany in this year that also saw the overthrow of several monarchies in France and Central Europe.
The nationalist leader Lajos Kossuth ["ko-SHOOT"] led this country's 1848 revolution. After that revolution's defeat, this country was organized into a "dual monarchy" with neighboring Austria.

697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bde.question: In this opera’s second act, the love duet “Wie sie fassen, wie sie lassen” is interrupted by a loud shriek and a shout of “Rette dich!” or “Save yourself!” In the third act, the male protagonist’s servant Kurwenal and his courtier Melot are both killed. In this opera’s final scene, a character asks Brangäne and King (*) Marke if they see how “softly and gently” her lover’s dead body shines in her “Liebestod.” The prelude of this opera opens with a dissonant (read slowly) F, B, D-sharp, G-sharp chord, which is often named for its male protagonist. For 10 points, a magic potion brings the title lovers together in what opera by Richard Wagner?
697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bde.question_sanitized: In this opera's second act, the love duet "Wie sie fassen, wie sie lassen" is interrupted by a loud shriek and a shout of "Rette dich!" or "Save yourself!" In the third act, the male protagonist's servant Kurwenal and his courtier Melot are both killed. In this opera's final scene, a character asks Brangane and King (*) Marke if they see how "softly and gently" her lover's dead body shines in her "Liebestod." The prelude of this opera opens with a dissonant (read slowly) F, B, D-sharp, G-sharp chord, which is often named for its male protagonist. For 10 points, a magic potion brings the title lovers together in what opera by Richard Wagner?
697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bde.answer_sanitized: Tristan und Isolde [or Tristan and Isolde]

697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bfc.leadin: A term meaning “living-togetherness” was coined to describe a period of coexistence between religious groups in this country’s history. For 10 points each:
697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38bfc.leadin_sanitized: A term meaning "living-togetherness" was coined to describe a period of coexistence between religious groups in this country's history. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this country, where the Muslim-controlled region of al-Andalus was slowly diminished by christian kingdoms in a period known as the Reconquista.

The Reconquista officially ended with the capture of this city, the capital of the Nasrid Kingdom, in 1492. This city’s last ruler, Boabdil, was forced to surrender it and the Alhambra Palace to Ferdinand and Isabel.
Most historians regard this battle as the turning point of the Reconquista. An Almohad army led by Muhammad al-Nasir was defeated by Alfonso VII at this 1212 battle.
parts_sanitized: Name this country, where the Muslim-controlled region of al-Andalus was slowly diminished by christian kingdoms in a period known as the Reconquista.

Most historians regard this battle as the turning point of the Reconquista. An Almohad army led by Muhammad al-Nasir was defeated by Alfonso VII at this 1212 battle.
answers: Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa [or Battle of Al-Uqab]

697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38c09.question: This artist depicted the donor offering the altarpiece itself to St. Peter in his Stefaneschi Altarpiece. 697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38c09.answer_sanitized: Giotto [or Giotto di Bondone; or Giottus]

697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38c0b.question: This author wrote a play based on the real life Helen Martins, who created The Owl House sculpture garden. In a play by this author, the photographer Styles takes a picture of the title character, who steals the identity of a dead man he finds in an alley so that he can remain in town. In a play this author developed with members of the The Serpent Players, John and Winston rehearse for a performance of Sophocles’ Antigone while in an unnamed (*) prison. This author wrote a play in which the title character reminisces about flying a kite, while his Black servants Sam and Willie practice ballroom dancing. 697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38c0b.question_sanitized: This author wrote a play based on the real life Helen Martins, who created The Owl House sculpture garden. This author wrote a play in which the title character reminisces about flying a kite, while his Black servants Sam and Willie practice ballroom dancing. For 10 points, name this South African playwright of The Island and Master Harold... and the Boys.
697ffb6a55785d7f5ce38c0b.answer: Athol Fugard (The first two plays are The Road to Mecca and Sizwe Banzi Is Dead.)
